qa_pairs.question: What is GROMACS-Plumed and how do users load it for simulations on CPUs or GPUs using the StdEnv/2023 environment?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS-Plumed modules are versions of GROMACS that have been modified with PLUMED, an open-source library for free energy calculations, to enable meta-dynamics simulations. For instance, to run GROMACS-Plumed version 2023.5 on CPUs in the StdEnv/2023 environment, you would use `module load StdEnv/2023 gcc/12.3 openmpi/4.1.5 gromacs-plumed/2023.5`. For GPU support with the same version, you would load `StdEnv/2023 gcc/12.3 openmpi/4.1.5 cuda/12.2 gromacs-plumed/2023.5`. It is also important to note that GROMACS versions 2025 and newer now feature native PLUMED support, allowing its use after loading any `plumed` module.

qa_pairs.question: What is GROMACS-Colvars, and what is the significant change regarding its integration into GROMACS as of version 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: GROMACS-Colvars is a software module for molecular simulation programs that adds capabilities for collective variables. These capabilities include applying biasing potentials, calculating potentials-of-mean-force (PMFs), and utilizing enhanced sampling methods such as Adaptive Biasing Force (ABF) and metadynamics. As of GROMACS version 2024, the Colvars library has been added directly to the official GROMACS releases, meaning a separate patched version is no longer required to use this functionality.
